questions.question: It takes you 46 seconds to fly between two adjacent VOR radials at 87 knots groundspeed. Assuming the winds are calm, how long would it take you to fly to the VOR?
questions.answerKeyType: Time Between Radials; Time to VOR

questions.question: Reference the previous question. KOSH is on MSN radial 031° and KJVL is on MSN radial 155°. After reaching KJVL, how much time would it take Jack if he now turned to fly to MSN?
questions.context: Referenced question 9: Jack took off out of KOSH on his way to KJVL, on a course of 195° cruising across the ground at 72 KTS. Jill takes off in the opposite direction going from KJVL to KOSH on the 84.5 NM journey getting a ground speed of 110 KTS. (Wind is 220° at 21 KTS and both airplanes fly at 91 KTAS.) At what point will they pass each other?

questions.question: Not considering wind, if you flew between 2 radials in 26 seconds at 97 knots, how long would it take to turn and fly 21 NM directly to the station?

Referencing the previous question, two waypoints along your straight-in final are identified by VOR radials. The first (final approach point) is at the 063° radial, and the second (missed approach point) is at the 079° radial. If your DME reads 24 nm at the final approach point, how long will it take you to reach the missed approach point?
questions.context: Referenced question 17: You are flying an approach to runway 16 at 103 KTAS, and the winds are 200° at 16 kts. What descent rate will keep you on the 4.8° descent angle?

questions.question: In straight & level flight your course passes by JFK VOR. First you cross radial 062° at 23:56:24Z and then radial 136° at 00:06:42Z. At 142 KTS ground speed, what is your time to the VOR if you turned to fly directly to it?
You travel between two bearings, from 087° bearing to 122° bearing, in 42 minutes at 35 NM. What is your time to the station?

The GPS-equipped airplane at the FBO was already rented. Now you're stuck with the old, steam gauge bird with VORs for your cross-country flight. You are 27 DME from RQZ and cross the 190° radial at 1957Z. You cross the 245° radial at 2011Z. What time will you reach your destination if you are flying across the radials towards it? It is 78 statute miles away.
You are flying at 115 kts. If it takes you 9 minutes to fly from the 073° radial to the 087° radial, how long would it take you to fly directly to the station?
If you cross the 163° radial at 1248Z and the 171° radial at 1253Z how long would it take to fly directly to the VOR at a ground speed of 125 KTS?
Using information from the previous question, if the pilot turned directly toward the station at the second radial, how long would it take before he flew over?
questions.context: Referenced question 21: A pilot flying along a straight course at 135 knots takes note of his time passing between two radials. What is the distance between the radials if the first one is passed at 17:22:18 and the second at 17:27:06? The radials are 23 degrees apart.

questions.question: As you are flying along during a cross country you decide to check some bearings off a nearby VOR. At 1705Z you cross the 115° radial. You take your next time at 1718Z when you cross the 155° radial. How long would it take you to fly directly to the VOR?
You cross 8° of radials from a VOR in 4.5 minutes. How long will it take for you to fly directly to the VOR?
A pilot inbound on the 360° radial of a VOR turns 20° left and flies for 8 minutes. When the pilot crosses the 020° radial they turn inbound again. How long will it take to fly to the VOR?
If you are at 199 NM from the VOR traveling at 85 mph for 2.5 minutes, how many bearings will you fly across?
Using the above information, what is your time to the station?
questions.context: Referenced question 29: If you are at 199 NM from the VOR traveling at 85 mph for 2.5 minutes, how many bearings will you fly across?

questions.question: Not considering wind, if you flew between 2 radials in 4 minutes at 105 knots, how long would it take to fly 21 nm directly to the station?
A pilot tunes his ADF to the nearest NDB and notes a bearing change of 12° after 2 minutes 18 seconds. How long would it take to turn and fly directly to the NDB at 93 knots?

You have determined that the winds are not as forecast. You need to calculate your actual groundspeed. You are nearing the Williamsport VOR and decide to tune it in. You are 34 nm from the facility and cross the 135° radial at 11:43:57 local. At 11:57:43 you cross the 085° radial. You still have 168 sm to your destination. What time will you arrive?
What is your groundspeed?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 5-7. You are flying between NDB bearings. You cross the 269° bearing at 11:48:42 and then cross the 279° bearing 11:50:54. Your distance to the station is 37 kilometers.

questions.question: Referencing the previous question, what is your time to the station?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 5-7. You are flying between NDB bearings. You cross the 269? bearing at 11:48:42 and then cross the 279? bearing 11:50:54. Your distance to the station is 37 kilometers. [[DIVIDER]] Referenced question 5: What is your groundspeed?

questions.question: You cross the DEN VOR radial 159 at 2026 zulu and cross the 145 radial at 2027:27 zulu. Your DME shows 47.5 nm. What is your groundspeed?
You fly across Radial 342 and then Radial 018 at a ground speed of 115 kts. If it takes you 22 minutes to turn and fly to the station, how long will it take you to pass between the radials?
You cross the 087- radial from the DJB VOR at 11:15. You cross the 101- radial at 11:19:30. How many minutes flying time are you from the VOR at a groundspeed of 146 kts?
You cross the 078 radial from the DJB VOR at 2315. Traveling clockwise you cross the 101 radial at 2319:30. How many minutes flying time are you from the VOR at a groundspeed of 138 kts?
Refer to the previous problem. How long would in take you to get to OOD if the wind is calm?
questions.context: Referenced question 20: You are flying around the OOD VORTAC and note 13° of bearing change in 5 minutes. If your groundspeed is 92 mph, how far from the station are you?

questions.question: An airplane is experiencing an 8° bearing change every 47 seconds. If it is 26 SM from the station, how long will it take to reach the station?

One day you are flying an Airbus A380 and an FAA Inspector is onboard giving you a line check. The inspector remembers you from a previous conversation and asks if you remember the time between radials equation. You do and he asks, "If I am 49 nautical miles from a VOR and I cross the 210° radial at 14:26:34 and the 219° radial at 14:28:28, what is my groundspeed?
If it takes 12 minutes to cross from radial 142° to radial 166°, what is the time to station?
